attachment.content:
- example-skill: Skill: example-skill
- odoo-18: Master index for Odoo 18 guides. This file provides a quick reference to find the appropriate detailed guide for each topic. Use this as an index to locate specific guides when working with Odoo 18 code. - Finding the appropriate guide for an Odoo 18 task,Understanding module structure and conventions,Looking up best practices for specific Odoo features
- brain-dump: /brain-dump - ADHD Brain Processor
- checkpoint: Checkpoint (GSD Phase Transition)
- end-work: End Work Session
- implement: /implement - Context-Engineered Implementation Phase
- learn: Learn - Capture Knowledge
- plan: /plan - Context-Engineered Planning Phase
- pulse: Pulse Check (Daily Work Rhythm)
- quick-commit: Quick Commit
- README: Commands
- reminder: /reminder - In-Session Action Capture
- research: /research - Context-Engineered Discovery Phase
- start-work: Start Work Session
- status: Status Check
- validate-work: Validate Work
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Configure cron job to run every 20 minutes

content.text: I'll check the current cron configuration and update it to run every 20 minutes.

content.content:
1	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
2	<odoo>
3	    <data>
4	        <record id="ir_cron_reorder_main_menus" model="ir.cron">
5	            <field name="name">Reorder Main Menus</field>
6	            <field name="model_id" ref="model_main_menu_priority"/>
7	            <field name="state">code</field>
8	            <field name="code">model.cron_reorder_main_menus()</field>
9	            <field name="interval_number">1</field>
10	            <field name="interval_type">days</field>
11	            <field name="active" eval="True"/>
12	            <field name="nextcall" eval="(DateTime.now() + timedelta(days=1)).replace(hour=6, minute=0, second=0)"/>
13	            <field name="user_id" ref="base.user_root"/>
14	        </record>
15	    </data>
16	</odoo>
17

file.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<odoo>
    <data>
        <record id="ir_cron_reorder_main_menus" model="ir.cron">
            <field name="name">Reorder Main Menus</field>
            <field name="model_id" ref="model_main_menu_priority"/>
            <field name="state">code</field>
            <field name="code">model.cron_reorder_main_menus()</field>
            <field name="interval_number">1</field>
            <field name="interval_type">days</field>
            <field name="active" eval="True"/>
            <field name="nextcall" eval="(DateTime.now() + timedelta(days=1)).replace(hour=6, minute=0, second=0)"/>
            <field name="user_id" ref="base.user_root"/>
        </record>
    </data>
</odoo>

content.text: The cron is currently set to run daily. I'll update it to run every 20 minutes.

            <field name="nextcall" eval="(DateTime.now() + timedelta(days=1)).replace(hour=6, minute=0, second=0)"/>
input.new_string:

            <field name="active" eval="True"/>
            <field name="nextcall" eval="DateTime.now() + timedelta(minutes=20)"/>

toolUseResult.originalFile:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<odoo>
    <data>
        <record id="ir_cron_reorder_main_menus" model="ir.cron">
            <field name="name">Reorder Main Menus</field>
            <field name="model_id" ref="model_main_menu_priority"/>
            <field name="state">code</field>
            <field name="code">model.cron_reorder_main_menus()</field>
            <field name="interval_number">1</field>
            <field name="interval_type">days</field>
            <field name="active" eval="True"/>
            <field name="nextcall" eval="(DateTime.now() + timedelta(days=1)).replace(hour=6, minute=0, second=0)"/>
            <field name="user_id" ref="base.user_root"/>
        </record>
    </data>
</odoo>

content.text: Updated the cron job at `main_menu_reorder/data/ir_cron_data.xml:9-12` to run every 20 minutes instead of daily. The next call time is now set to 20 minutes from installation rather than 6am the next day.

content: Updated the cron job in main_menu_reorder/data/ir_cron_data.xml to run every 20 minutes instead of daily. The change is complete and ready to deploy whenever you update the module in Odoo. (disable recaps in /config)

aiTitle: Configure cron job to run every 20 minutes

content.text: I'll restructure the menu to have a parent without action, and add the reports as a submenu alongside the configuration menu.

input.old_string:
<menuitem id="menu_project_daily_root"
              name="Daily Reports"
              parent="project.menu_main_pm"
              action="action_project_daily"
              sequence="10"/>
input.new_string:
<!-- Parent Menu (no action) -->
    <menuitem id="menu_project_daily_root"
              name="Daily Reports"
              parent="project.menu_main_pm"
              sequence="10"/>

    <!-- Reports Submenu -->
    <menuitem id="menu_project_daily_reports"
              name="Reports"
              parent="menu_project_daily_root"
              action="action_project_daily"
              sequence="1"/>

content.content: The file X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\project_dailys\views\project_daily_views.xml has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

<menuitem id="menu_project_daily_root"
              name="Daily Reports"
              parent="project.menu_main_pm"
              action="action_project_daily"
              sequence="10"/>
toolUseResult.newString:
<!-- Parent Menu (no action) -->
    <menuitem id="menu_project_daily_root"
              name="Daily Reports"
              parent="project.menu_main_pm"
              sequence="10"/>

    <!-- Reports Submenu -->
    <menuitem id="menu_project_daily_reports"
              name="Reports"
              parent="menu_project_daily_root"
              action="action_project_daily"
              sequence="1"/>

content.text: Restructured the menu at `project_daily_views.xml:249-258`. The "Daily Reports" is now a parent menu without action, and the reports action is now a "Reports" submenu (sequence 1). This allows the configuration submenu to sit alongside it.

content: Updated two files: changed the main_menu_reorder cron job to run every 20 minutes instead of daily, and restructured project_dailys menu so Daily Reports is a parent with Reports as a submenu to accommodate the configuration menu. Both changes are complete. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text:
Updated all monetary field cells in the BOM summary table. Each field is now wrapped in a `<div style="text-align: right;">` which should force right alignment regardless of the widget's internal styling.

For the $ symbol: The monetary widget automatically displays the currency symbol based on the `company_currency` field. If the $ isn't showing:
1. Verify the company's currency is set to USD in Settings → Companies
2. Make sure the `company_currency` field has a value on the project record

After updating Odoo and refreshing the page, all numeric columns should be right-aligned and display the currency symbol if USD is configured.

content.text:
Cleaned up the table cells by removing div wrappers and using Bootstrap's `text-end` class directly on the `<td>` elements. This is the proper way to handle alignment in Odoo 18 (which uses Bootstrap 5).

Changes made:
- All numeric columns now use `class="text-end"` on the `<td>` elements
- Removed unnecessary div wrappers
- Kept `font-weight: bold;` in the style attribute for TOTAL and Profit % rows

After updating the `project_bom` module and refreshing the page, all numeric columns should be right-aligned. The $ currency symbol should display automatically if your company currency is configured as USD.

content: Working on right-aligning numeric columns in the BOM summary table and adding currency symbols. Tried inline styles and div wrappers without success, now using Bootstrap text-end class on table cells which should work properly in Odoo 18. Update the project_bom module and refresh to test the alignment.
